pgn_features.notes: string

motifs.name: Alekhine central storm to Lucena
motifs.description: White overextends central pawns against Alekhine; queens and minors are exchanged, leading to a rook + outside passed pawn vs rook endgame.

pgn_features.notes: Game must feature early central pawn advances (e4-e5, d4) and large-scale minor piece exchanges.

motifs.name: Scandinavian queen trade to Philidor rook defence
motifs.description: Early queen trade and symmetric pawn structure steers the game into a rook vs rook+pawn endgame of Philidor type.

pgn_features.notes: 1.e4 d5 followed by exd5 Qxd5 and an early queen swap; game simplifies quickly.

fen_pattern_rules: material_white == {K:1,R:1} and material_black in [{K:1,R:1,P:1},{K:1,R:1,2P:1}] or vice_versa

motifs.name: Dutch dark-square clamp to opposite-color bishops
motifs.description: Dutch structures with ...f5-e6-d6 lead to exchanges of queens and rooks, leaving opposite-color bishops and fixed pawn chains.

pgn_features.notes: Typical manoeuvres: ...f5, ...e6, ...d6, piece play around dark squares, then heavy-piece exchanges.

motifs.name: Philidor simplification to king+pawn races
motifs.description: Black's compact Philidor setup leads to mass piece exchanges and a pure king+pawn ending where opposition and key squares decide.

pgn_features.notes: Quiet manoeuvring from a Philidor structure with many trades and symmetric central pawns.

motifs.name: Nimzowitsch simplification to rule-of-the-square pawn race
motifs.description: Unorthodox piece placement leads to unusual pawn trades; one side obtains a far advanced passed pawn vs lone king, invoking the rule of the square.

pgn_features.notes: 1.e4 Nc6 with flexible development and later piece exchanges; eventually queens and most pieces disappear.

fen_pattern_rules: material_white in [{K:1,P:1},{K:1,2P:1}] and material_black == {K:1} or vice_versa

motifs.name: Grünfeld central liquidation to Réti pawn race
motifs.description: Grünfeld structure where central pawns are exchanged, pieces come off, and the game simplifies into a king-and-pawn race resembling the Réti motif.

pgn_features.notes: Look for early central exchanges and heavy-piece trades leading to a pure pawn endgame.

motifs.name: Ruy Lopez maneuvering to wrong-color bishop ending
motifs.description: Closed Ruy Lopez where heavy pieces are exchanged, leaving bishops vs pawns and a rook pawn whose promotion square is of the opposite color of the bishop.

pgn_features.notes: Typical long maneuvering Ruy with later exchanges leaving bishop + rook pawn vs king.

motifs.name: Moscow simplification to Saavedra-type rook ending
motifs.description: Moscow Sicilian with early Bb5+ leading to a simplified rook-and-pawn vs rook endgame with tactical winning motifs reminiscent of Saavedra.

pgn_features.notes: Anti-Sicilian with early check, queens off early, and a sharp rook+pawn vs rook finish.

motifs.name: Caro-Kann Advance to Lasker rook defense
motifs.description: Advance Caro-Kann structure where Black defends a slightly worse rook-and-pawn endgame using active-rook drawing techniques similar to Lasker’s defense.

pgn_features.notes: Caro-Kann pawn chain, queens off, then Black holds a worse rook+ pawn endgame by activity.

motifs.name: Winawer imbalance to Vancura rook defense
motifs.description: Winawer positions with queenside pawn imbalances that eventually simplify into a rook + rook pawn vs rook ending where the defender uses a Vancura-style third-rank defense.

pgn_features.notes: French Winawer pawn structure, then heavy-piece trades, then rook+rook pawn vs rook with side-checks.

motifs.name: Closed Sicilian to Bishop vs Knight ending
motifs.description: Slow kingside attack with piece trades leaving bishop vs knight structures.

pgn_features.notes: Closed center, many minor piece trades, queens exchanged by move ~25.

motifs.name: QGA simplification to two knights vs pawn
motifs.description: Central exchanges leading to two knights vs pawn technical ending.

pgn_features.notes: Open central files with heavy simplification.

motifs.name: Petroff to shoulder zugzwang king+pawn ending
motifs.description: Symmetrical exchanges drifting into king+pawn shoulder zugzwang positions.

pgn_features.notes: Petroff symmetry, early queen trades, many central pawn exchanges.

motifs.name: 1.d4 e6 systems to Kling-Horwitz defensive setup
motifs.description: Blocked pawn chains with rooks behind pawn structures yielding Kling-Horwitz defenses.

pgn_features.notes: Closed center with heavy-piece simplification.

motifs.name: Symmetrical English to opposite-colour bishops
motifs.description: Symmetrical English where piece trades and pawn structure create OCB endgames.

pgn_features.notes: Multiple minor exchanges, bishops remaining on opposite colours.

motifs.name: Najdorf complications to R+B vs R+N endgame
motifs.description: Sharp Najdorf middlegame with piece trades leading to R+B vs R+N imbalance.

pgn_features.notes: Complex middlegame resolving into piece-imbalanced rook endgame.

motifs.name: Berlin endgame fortress
motifs.description: Berlin queenless endgame evolving into fortress-type positions.

pgn_features.notes: Early queen trade, symmetrical structure, fortress motifs.

motifs.name: Slav simplification to Stamma-type minor piece endgame
motifs.description: Solid Slav structures that trade to minor-piece Stamma-like studies.

pgn_features.notes: Queenside pawn majority and minor-piece manoeuvring.

motifs.name: Nimzo-Indian Rubinstein to Troitzky-line style ending
motifs.description: Nimzo Rubinstein where central structure and minor-piece trades lead to two knights vs pawn themes.

pgn_features.notes: Typical Nimzo pressure with eventual minor-piece vs pawn ending.

motifs.name: KID classical to Cochrane-type rook vs pawns defence
motifs.description: Kingside attacks resolving into rook vs two connected passed pawns, Cochrane-style.

pgn_features.notes: Sharp KID attack liquidating to R vs 2P.
